title: Auto Debt-Free Certificate Cost Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate the cost of a debt-free vehicle certificate (libre deuda) for selling your car in Argentina. Fees, requirements & processing times.
intro: Official vehicle transactions in Argentina involve government fees that vary by province, with different processing times and documentation requirements. Understanding these costs upfront saves you trips and unexpected expenses. A debt-free auto certificate (libre deuda) proves your vehicle has no outstanding traffic violations or liens—essential for selling or transferring ownership. Costs, timelines, and requirements are updated for 2026.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Processing fee + administrative costs**. Apply the calculator with your province details to get your exact amount.

Budget for vehicle sale or transfer expenses

explanation:
A debt-free auto certificate (libre deuda) is an official document confirming your vehicle has no outstanding traffic violations, parking fines, or registered liens. This is required to sell or transfer vehicle ownership in Argentina.

## How It Works

Each Argentine province sets its own processing fee for this certificate. The calculator estimates your total cost based on your province selection. Processing fees typically range from $10,000 to $25,000 ARS depending on location.

## Important Notes

This calculator provides an estimate for planning purposes. For official details about your specific situation, contact your provincial vehicle registry (DNRPA equivalent) or visit argentina.gob.ar. Fees are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is a debt-free auto certificate?
faq.a: It's an official document proving your vehicle has no outstanding traffic violations, fines, or liens—required to sell or transfer ownership.

faq.q: Do I need this certificate to sell my car?
faq.a: Yes, it's mandatory for any vehicle sale or transfer of ownership in Argentina.
Costs vary by province, typically between $10,000–$25,000 ARS. Use this calculator for your specific province.

faq.q: How long does it take to get one?
faq.a: Processing typically takes 1–5 business days online, longer if you apply in person.
Many provinces offer online applications through their vehicle registry (DNRPA). Check your provincial government website.
Usually your vehicle's registration documents, proof of ownership, DNI (national ID), and proof of address.

faq.q: What if my car has outstanding debts or fines?
faq.a: You must settle all fines and traffic violations before applying for a debt-free certificate.

faq.q: How long is the certificate valid?
faq.a: Typically 30 days from issuance. Plan your sale to avoid re-applying.
